overview: Comprehensive correction plan for backend API stability, security, and reliability

issues.title: In-memory DB data loss on restart
issues.description: All user and translation data lost when server restarts; not suitable for production or testing persistence
issues.impact: Critical - users lose all data on deployment or crash
issues.root_cause: Using mongodb-memory-server which stores data in RAM, not persistent storage

steps: Migrate to MongoDB Atlas (cloud) or local MongoDB instance
Update .env to point to real MongoDB URI: MONGODB_URI=mongodb+srv://user:pass@cluster.mongodb.net/signconnect
Remove USE_IN_MEMORY_DB flag or set to false in production
Test connection with real database before deployment
fix.codeExample: MONGODB_URI=mongodb+srv://<user>:<password>@cluster.mongodb.net/signconnect?retryWrites=true&w=majority

issues.title: No database indexes on frequently queried fields
issues.description: Email lookups (login, duplicate check) and user lookups perform full collection scans
issues.impact: Performance degradation as user count grows; O(n) queries instead of O(log n)
issues.root_cause: User model has no indexes defined on searchable fields

steps: Update User.js: add index to email field
Update Translation.js: add index to userId field for faster user translation retrieval
Run database migration to create indexes on existing collections
fix.codeExample: email: { type: String, required: true, unique: true, lowercase: true, trim: true, index: true }

issues.title: No schema validation for data integrity
issues.description: Invalid data accepted at database layer (negative confidence, null email, empty text)
issues.impact: Data corruption; translation confidence could be -999, invalid emails stored
issues.root_cause: Mongoose schemas define fields but not constraints or ranges

steps: User.js: add email format validation and required checks
Translation.js: add confidence range (0-1), non-empty text validation
Add custom validators for business logic (e.g., email length limits)
fix.codeExample: confidence: { type: Number, default: 0, min: 0, max: 1, validate: { validator: (v) => v >= 0 && v <= 1 } }

issues.title: Orphaned translation records on user deletion
issues.description: If user is deleted, their translations remain in database with dangling references
issues.impact: Database bloat; orphaned records never cleaned up; potential data exposure
issues.root_cause: No foreign key constraints or cascade delete rules

fix.type: Implement cascade delete or add user deletion endpoint
steps: Create DELETE /api/auth/user endpoint to delete user and all their translations
Use Mongoose middleware to delete translations before user deletion
Alternatively, use MongoDB transaction to delete user and associated translations atomically
fix.codeExample: userSchema.pre('deleteOne', async function(next) { await Translation.deleteMany({ userId: this._id }); next(); });

issues.title: No password complexity validation
issues.description: Weak passwords accepted (e.g., 'pass', '123456', single character)
issues.impact: Account compromise; weak passwords easy to brute force
issues.root_cause: No password strength checks in register endpoint

steps: Require minimum 8 characters
Require uppercase, lowercase, number, special character
Check against common weak passwords list
Reject if < 10 entropy score
fix.codeExample: const passwordRegex = /^(?=.*[a-z])(?=.*[A-Z])(?=.*\d)(?=.*[@$!%*?&])[A-Za-z\d@$!%*?&]{8,}$/; if (!passwordRegex.test(password)) return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must be 8+ chars...' });

issues.title: JWT tokens have long expiry with no refresh mechanism
issues.description: 7-day token expiry is too long; no way to revoke tokens; no token refresh endpoint
issues.impact: Stolen tokens valid for 7 days; no way to force logout; increased session hijacking risk
issues.root_cause: Basic JWT implementation without refresh token pattern

steps: Create short-lived access token (15 minutes) and long-lived refresh token (7 days)
Store refresh token in database (table: RefreshToken { token, userId, expiresAt })
Create POST /api/auth/refresh endpoint to exchange refresh token for new access token
Validate refresh token against database (prevent use after logout)
Return both tokens on register/login
fix.codeExample: const accessToken = jwt.sign({ userId }, secret, { expiresIn: '15m' }); const refreshToken = jwt.sign({ userId }, secret, { expiresIn: '7d' }); await RefreshToken.create({ token: refreshToken, userId });

issues.title: No token revocation/blacklist mechanism
issues.description: Users can't force logout; stolen tokens work until 7-day expiry; tokens can't be revoked on account compromise
issues.impact: No way to prevent token reuse after logout; account takeover risk
issues.root_cause: No logout implementation; JWT inherently can't be revoked without DB lookup

fix.type: Implement token blacklist or refresh token invalidation
steps: Option 1: Store revoked tokens in database (TokenBlacklist { token, expiresAt })
Option 2: Add 'jti' (JWT ID) to token and check against blacklist on requests
Option 3: Invalidate all refresh tokens for user on logout
Create POST /api/auth/logout endpoint to add token to blacklist
Check blacklist in authenticate middleware before allowing request
fix.codeExample: router.post('/logout', authenticate, async (req, res) => { await TokenBlacklist.create({ token: req.headers.authorization.split(' ')[1], expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000) }); res.json({ message: 'Logged out' }); });

issues.title: JWT_SECRET stored in readable .env file
issues.description: Secret stored in plaintext .env file; if repo or server compromised, all tokens can be forged
issues.impact: Critical - Full session hijacking if .env leaked
issues.root_cause: Using local .env instead of secure secrets management

steps: Create secret in chosen vault
Update backend to fetch JWT_SECRET from vault at startup
Remove JWT_SECRET from .env file
Add fallback only for local development using insecure flag
Rotate secret annually or after suspected breach
fix.codeExample: const jwtSecret = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' ? await getFromVault('jwt-secret') : process.env.JWT_SECRET;

issues.title: No brute-force protection on auth endpoints
issues.description: Attackers can repeatedly try login/register with different passwords without limits
issues.impact: Account takeover; credential stuffing attacks; DOS through auth endpoint spam
issues.root_cause: Generic rate limiter (120 req/min) not auth-specific

steps: Create stricter limiter: 5 attempts per 15 minutes per email address
Create stricter limiter: 10 register attempts per IP per hour
Store failed attempts in cache (Redis) with expiry
Return 429 status and 'Too many attempts' message
Lock account temporarily after 10 failed attempts (optional)
fix.codeExample: const authLimiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15*60*1000, max: 5, keyGenerator: (req) => req.body?.email || req.ip, skip: (req) => req.method === 'GET' });

issues.title: CORS allows all origins
issues.description: Anyone from any domain can call your API; not restricted to front-end domains
issues.impact: Cross-site attacks; unauthorized API usage from external sites
issues.root_cause: corsOptions.origin set to '*'

steps: Define allowed origins in .env: ALLOWED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:3000,https://yourapp.com
Update corsOptions.origin to whitelist only these domains
Disallow credentials if origin is not trusted
Test that cross-origin requests from other domains are rejected
fix.codeExample: origin: (process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS || 'http://localhost:3000').split(','), credentials: true

issues.title: No API versioning
issues.description: Routes are /api/auth/register; if API changes, all clients break
issues.impact: Can't make breaking changes; stuck with old API contracts
issues.root_cause: Routes not versioned

steps: Update routes to /api/v1/auth/register, /api/v1/translations/*
Create new version endpoint /api/v2/* when breaking changes needed
Support multiple API versions during transition
Document version deprecation timeline
fix.codeExample: app.use('/api/v1/auth', authRoutes); app.use('/api/v1/translations', translationRoutes);

issues.title: Inconsistent error response format
issues.description: Errors return different formats: sometimes 'message' field, no error codes, no details field
issues.impact: Client code hard to parse; can't distinguish error types programmatically
issues.root_cause: Ad-hoc error response format

steps: Create error response standard: { code: string, message: string, details?: object, timestamp: ISO8601 }
Define error codes: AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS, DB_CONFLICT, VALIDATION_ERROR, etc.
Create error handler middleware to wrap all errors in standard format
Document all possible error codes in API docs
fix.codeExample: { code: 'AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS', message: 'Invalid email or password', timestamp: '2026-03-18T...' }

issues.title: Generic 500 errors without details
issues.description: All server errors return 500 with error.message; client can't distinguish user error from bug
issues.impact: Hard to debug; users see unhelpful 'Internal server error' messages
issues.root_cause: Catch-all error handler logs but returns same 500 for all errors

fix.type: Add detailed error logging and categorization
steps: Log full error stack traces server-side with context (user, endpoint, request data)
Return safe error message to client ('Something went wrong'), log ID to stderr
Return 400 for validation/user errors, 500 only for true server errors
Integrate Sentry/DataDog to alert on 500 errors
Create error.log file to track all errors for debugging
fix.codeExample: console.error(`[ERROR-${uuid}]`, err); res.status(500).json({ code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR', message: 'Something went wrong', errorId: uuid });

issues.title: No request validation; XSS/injection risks
issues.description: Invalid inputs not checked before database; users could inject scripts in inputText
issues.impact: XSS attacks; invalid data in database; NoSQL injection possible
issues.root_cause: No input sanitization or validation library

steps: Install validator or joi library
Create validation middleware for all routes
Validate email format, password length, text fields not empty
Sanitize strings to remove scripts/HTML tags
Reject requests failing validation with 400 status
fix.codeExample: const { email, password } = req.body; if (!validator.isEmail(email)) return res.status(400).json({ code: 'INVALID_EMAIL', message: 'Invalid email format' });

issues.title: No API documentation
issues.description: No Swagger/OpenAPI docs; developers don't know endpoint contracts, request/response formats
issues.impact: Slow onboarding; integration errors; can't auto-generate client SDKs
issues.root_cause: No documentation tooling integrated

steps: Install swagger-jsdoc and swagger-ui-express
Add JSDoc comments to all route handlers documenting request/response
Generate Swagger JSON at /api/docs/swagger.json
Host Swagger UI at /api/docs
Update documentation on deployment
fix.codeExample:
/**
 * @swagger
 * /api/v1/auth/register:
 *   post:
 *     requestBody:
 *       content:
 *         application/json:
 *           schema:
 *             properties:
 *               email:
 *                 type: string
 */

issues.title: No HTTPS enforcement in production
issues.description: If deployed without HTTPS, credentials and tokens sent in plaintext
issues.impact: Man-in-the-middle attacks; credential sniffing; session hijacking
issues.root_cause: No HTTPS redirect middleware

steps: Add middleware to redirect HTTP to HTTPS if NODE_ENV=production
Set HSTS header to force HTTPS: res.set('Strict-Transport-Security', 'max-age=31536000')
Use Helmet middleware to set security headers
Provision SSL certificate (Let's Encrypt free for public domains)
Test HTTPS enforcement with curl -I https://your-api.com
fix.codeExample: if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') { app.use((req, res, next) => { if (!req.secure) return res.redirect('https://' + req.headers.host + req.url); next(); }); }

issues.title: No request/response logging
issues.description: Can't debug issues, audit user actions, or track API usage patterns
issues.impact: Incidents hard to debug; no audit trail for compliance; can't optimize performance
issues.root_cause: No logging middleware

steps: Install Winston or Pino logger
Log every request: timestamp, method, path, user, status, response time
Log errors with full stack trace and request context
Rotate logs daily and archive old logs
Search logs in Kibana or similar for debugging
fix.codeExample: app.use((req, res, next) => { const start = Date.now(); res.on('finish', () => { logger.info({ timestamp: new Date(), method: req.method, path: req.path, userId: req.user?.userId, status: res.statusCode, duration: Date.now() - start }); }); next(); });

issues.title: POST /translations missing error handling
issues.description: Route lacks try/catch; if database error occurs, server crashes
issues.impact: Endpoint crashes instead of returning 500; server restart needed
issues.root_cause: No error handling in async route handler

steps: Add try/catch block in POST /translations
Validate input fields before database insert
Return 400 if validation fails, 500 if database fails
Log error details for debugging
fix.codeExample: try { ... } catch (err) { console.error('Create translation error:', err); res.status(500).json({ code: 'DB_ERROR', message: 'Failed to create translation' }); }

issues.title: Confidence field can be negative or >1
issues.description: Schema allows confidence field outside valid ML range [0, 1]
issues.impact: Invalid metric stored; can't use in ML pipelines; data quality issue
issues.root_cause: No schema validation on confidence field

steps: Update Translation schema: confidence { type: Number, min: 0, max: 1 }
Add request validation: reject if confidence not in [0, 1]
Add error message: 'Confidence must be between 0 and 1'
fix.codeExample: confidence: { type: Number, default: 0, min: 0, max: 1, validate: { validator: (v) => v >= 0 && v <= 1, message: 'Confidence out of range' } }

issues.title: GET /translations returns only 50 records
issues.description: User with large translation history can't retrieve all records; no pagination or filter support
issues.impact: User data incomplete; can't view full history; no way to export all translations
issues.root_cause: Hard-coded limit of 50; no pagination params

steps: Add query params: ?page=1&limit=20&sort=-createdAt
Implement skip/limit: skip = (page - 1) * limit
Return total count in response: { data: [...], total: 500, page: 1, limit: 20 }
Add createdAt index for sorting performance
fix.codeExample: const page = parseInt(req.query.page || 1); const limit = parseInt(req.query.limit || 20); const skip = (page - 1) * limit; const data = await Translation.find({userId}).skip(skip).limit(limit); const total = await Translation.countDocuments({userId});

issues.title: No PUT/DELETE endpoints for translations
issues.description: Users can't edit or delete translation records; only create and read
issues.impact: Data immutable; users can't fix mistakes; can't delete sensitive translations
issues.root_cause: Only POST and GET routes implemented

steps: Create PUT /api/v1/translations/:id endpoint to update inputText, outputText, confidence
Create DELETE /api/v1/translations/:id endpoint to delete single translation
Create DELETE /api/v1/translations endpoint to clear all user translations
Verify user ownership before allowing edit/delete
Return 404 if translation not found or user not owner
fix.codeExample: router.put('/:id', authenticate, async (req, res) => { const trans = await Translation.findById(req.params.id); if (!trans || trans.userId !== req.user.userId) return res.status(404).json({...}); await Translation.updateOne({_id: id}, req.body); res.json(trans); });

issues.title: Generic rate limiter too loose for auth endpoints
issues.description: 120 requests per minute is too generous for login/register; could allow brute force
issues.impact: Auth endpoints vulnerable to brute force attacks
issues.root_cause: Single rate limiter applied to all routes

steps: Create auth limiter: 5 attempts per 15 minutes per email
Create API limiter: 100 requests per minute for normal endpoints
Apply auth limiter only to /auth/login and /auth/register
Track by email address, not IP (more accurate for login attempts)
fix.codeExample: const authLimiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15*60*1000, max: 5, keyGenerator: (req) => req.body?.email || req.ip }); app.use('/api/auth/login', authLimiter);

issues.title: Rate limiting not IP-aware; vulnerable to DDoS
issues.description: Standard rate limiter counts globally, not per IP; DDoS from many IPs bypasses limits
issues.impact: DDoS attacks can overwhelm server
issues.root_cause: No per-IP tracking

fix.type: Add IP-based rate limiting with DDoS protection
steps: Track limits per IP address using Redis
Add stricter global limit: 1000 req/min total across all IPs
Cache IP reputation (block known bad IPs)
Use reverse proxy (Cloudflare, AWS WAF) for DDoS mitigation
fix.codeExample: const limiter = rateLimit({ store: new RedisStore({...}), keyGenerator: (req) => req.ip });

issues.title: Rate limiter counts failed login attempts poorly
issues.description: Legitimate user gets rate-limited after typos; limiter doesn't skip on success
issues.impact: Usability issue; frustrates users
issues.root_cause: Limiter counts all requests equally

fix.type: Skip rate limiter on successful authentication
steps: Add skipSuccessfulRequests: true to rate limiter
Only count failed attempts toward limit
Reset counter on successful login
fix.codeExample: const authLimiter = rateLimit({ skipSuccessfulRequests: true, skipFailedRequests: false });

issues.title: Hard-coded configuration values in source code
issues.description: PORT, JWT_EXPIRY, LOG_LEVEL hard-coded; can't change without code edit
issues.impact: Can't configure per environment; tight coupling
issues.root_cause: Settings not externalized to .env

steps: Add all hard-coded values to .env: PORT, JWT_EXPIRY, LOG_LEVEL, DB_TIMEOUT, etc.
Create .env.example with defaults and descriptions
Load config from .env at startup
Document all config variables in README
fix.codeExample: const jwt_expiry = process.env.JWT_EXPIRY || '7d'; const log_level = process.env.LOG_LEVEL || 'info';

issues.title: No environment validation at startup
issues.description: Missing required env vars causes cryptic errors at runtime instead of fast fail
issues.impact: Hard to debug; server starts but crashes when first request made
issues.root_cause: No validation middleware

steps: Create config validator function
Check all required vars present: JWT_SECRET, MONGODB_URI, NODE_ENV
Exit process with clear error message if missing
Run validation before starting server
fix.codeExample: const required = ['JWT_SECRET', 'MONGODB_URI']; for (const key of required) { if (!process.env[key]) { console.error(`❌ Missing required env var: ${key}`); process.exit(1); } }

issues.title: .env file committed to git (if no .gitignore)
issues.description: Secrets exposed in repo history if .env accidentally committed
issues.impact: Critical - JWT_SECRET accessible to anyone with repo access
issues.root_cause: No .gitignore rule for .env or accidental commit

steps: Verify .gitignore contains: .env, .env.local, .env.*.local
If already committed, use git-filter-repo to remove from history
Rotate JWT_SECRET if exposed
Commit only .env.example as template
fix.codeExample:
.env
.env.local
.env.*.local

issues.title: No separate configurations per environment
issues.description: Dev, staging, and production use same settings; can't set different log levels, timeouts, etc.
issues.impact: Can't optimize per environment; debug logging in production
issues.root_cause: Single .env file used for all environments

steps: Create .env.development, .env.staging, .env.production
Load file based on NODE_ENV: dotenv.config({ path: `.env.${process.env.NODE_ENV}` })
Override with system env vars if set
Document each environment's settings
fix.codeExample: const envFile = `.env.${process.env.NODE_ENV || 'development'}`; dotenv.config({ path: path.resolve(__dirname, envFile) });

issues.title: No error tracking or alerting
issues.description: Bugs only found when users report them; no automatic notifications
issues.impact: Slow incident response; hidden bugs in production
issues.root_cause: No error tracking service integrated

steps: Install Sentry SDK: npm install @sentry/node
Initialize Sentry in index.js with DSN from Sentry dashboard
Wrap routes with Sentry error handler
Set up Slack/email alerts for errors
fix.codeExample: const Sentry = require('@sentry/node'); Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN }); app.use(Sentry.Handlers.errorHandler());

issues.title: No response time tracking
issues.description: Unknown if API is slow; can't identify performance bottlenecks
issues.impact: Poor user experience; undetected performance regressions
issues.root_cause: No performance monitoring

fix.type: Add request duration tracking and monitoring
steps: Log response time for each request
Track percentiles: p50, p95, p99 response times
Alert if p95 response time > 500ms
Identify slow endpoints and optimize
fix.codeExample: const start = Date.now(); res.on('finish', () => { logger.info({ duration: Date.now() - start, method: req.method, path: req.path }); });

issues.title: No database query logging or optimization
issues.description: Can't see which queries are slow; can't optimize N+1 queries
issues.impact: Slow database operations; can't find bottlenecks
issues.root_cause: No query-level logging

fix.type: Enable Mongoose query logging and analysis
steps: Enable Mongoose debug mode: mongoose.set('debug', true)
Log all queries with execution time
Use MongoDB profiler to find slow queries (>100ms)
Identify and add missing indexes
Refactor N+1 queries with lean() or populate()
fix.codeExample: mongoose.set('debug', (collection, method, query, result, duration) => { if (duration > 100) console.warn(`Slow query: ${collection}.${method} (${duration}ms)`); });

issues.title: Health endpoint missing important metrics
issues.description: GET /health only returns 'ok'; doesn't show database status, memory, uptime
issues.impact: Can't determine if server is healthy before it fails
issues.root_cause: Minimal health check implementation

fix.type: Enhance health endpoint with detailed metrics
steps: Return database connection status
Include memory usage and uptime
Check that required services are reachable
Return 503 if not fully healthy
fix.codeExample: { status: 'ok', uptime: 3600, memory: { used: 50, total: 512 }, database: 'connected', timestamp: '2026-03-18T...' }

issues.title: Only happy-path tests implemented
issues.description: Tests only cover success cases; no edge cases, error cases, or boundary conditions
issues.impact: Bugs in error handling not caught; incomplete test coverage
issues.root_cause: Minimal test suite in test_auth.js

fix.type: Expand test coverage to include edge cases
steps: Add tests for invalid inputs: empty email, weak password, null fields
Add tests for error cases: duplicate email, wrong password, invalid token
Add tests for boundary conditions: max password length, min confidence
Test all error codes and status codes returned
Use Jest or Mocha for test framework
fix.codeExample: test('register rejects weak password', async () => { const res = await register({ email: 'user@test.com', password: 'weak' }); expect(res.status).toBe(400); });

issues.title: No load/performance testing
issues.description: Unknown if API scales under load; can't find bottlenecks
issues.impact: Production failures under high traffic; unknown capacity limits
issues.root_cause: No load testing suite

steps: Use Apache JMeter or Artillery for load testing
Simulate 1000 concurrent users hitting endpoints
Measure p50, p95, p99 response times
Measure memory/CPU under load
Identify bottlenecks and optimize
fix.codeExample: artillery run load-test.yml

issues.title: No security testing; XSS/injection risks unknown
issues.description: Potential XSS, SQL injection, timing attacks not tested
issues.impact: Security vulnerabilities undetected
issues.root_cause: No security test suite

steps: Use OWASP ZAP or Burp Suite for automated scans
Test for XSS by injecting <script> tags in text fields
Test for injection: inputText: 'hello\' OR '1'='1'
Test for timing attacks on login (constant-time comparison)
Verify rate limiting blocks brute force attempts

issues.title: No integration tests for full workflows
issues.description: Unknown if auth → translation flow works end-to-end
issues.impact: Integration failures not caught until production
issues.root_cause: Only unit tests for individual endpoints

steps: Test flow: register → login → create translation → read translation
Test auth failures: expired token, bad token, no token
Test permission checks: user can't delete another user's translations
Test cleanup: verify orphaned data not created
fix.codeExample: describe('Auth → Translations workflow', () => { test('user can register and create translation', async () => { ...register...create translation...verify }); });
